**Research summary:** External tools (snippet_search, web_search, cached page) returned no usable content for the amyloid-kinetics literature. I'll rely on the well-established Knowles/Cohen amyloid master-equation framework, which I know from domain knowledge: the fibril **mass concentration** M(t) evolves under moment closure as

  dM/dt = k⁺ m(t) P(t) (elongation) + monomer-dependent primary nucleation, with **P(t)** (aggregate number) fed by **secondary nucleation ∝ k₂ m(t) M(t)ⁿ²**. Eliminating P and closing moments yields a self-catalytic mass term that is **superlinear in M** — i.e., a growth contribution ∝ M² is the canonical signature that produces the sharp sigmoidal knee and the correct half-time/onset scaling. Inhibitors act multiplicatively to suppress these rate constants (delaying onset), preserving the cd=0 baseline.
